text: In this letter, we introduce a practical non-linear energy harvesting model and proposed an efficient resource allocation algorithm for simultaneous wireless information and power transfer (SWIPT) systems. Based on the non-linear energy harvesting model, the considered SWIPT system is formulated into a non-convex optimization problem to maximize the total energy harvested at the energy receivers (ERs) while satisfying the required signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) for the information receivers (IRs). To obtain the optimal solution, the non-convex objective function with sum-of-ratios structure is first reformulated into an equivalent subtractive form by using some mathematical manipulations and algebraic transformations, which leads to deriving an efficient iterative algorithm. The rank constraint in the SDPs involved in the iterations is addressed by utilizing the SDP relaxation method. Simulation results show that the incorporation of non-linear energy harvesting (EH) model significantly outperforms that of linear EH model.
